text: Hi, I placed an order yesterday for the Bordeaux case. Can you confirm if it'll arrive before Friday? Hosting a dinner that evening.
Hello Robert! Yes, your order shipped this morning via FedEx Priority. Estimated delivery is Thursday by 5pm. I'll send the tracking number shortly.
Perfect, thank you! Much appreciated.
Hi, I received the 2019 Bordeaux case I ordered last week but two of the bottles arrived damaged. I'd like to arrange a return or replacement please.
Hello Emily, so sorry to hear that! Could you send us a photo of the damaged bottles? We'll arrange a full replacement and collection at no cost to you.
Of course, I'll send the photos now. Thank you for sorting this so quickly!
Hi, this is Jack. Just wanted to confirm my tasting appointment for tomorrow at 3pm. Still good to go?
Hello Jack! Yes, absolutely confirmed for tomorrow, Tuesday 30th at 3:00pm. We have a lovely Bordeaux selection ready for you. 🍷
Perfect, looking forward to it. See you then!
Hi, could you tell me what your store hours are this week? Planning to stop by to pick up a few bottles.
Hello George! We're open Mon–Sat, 10am to 7pm, and Sunday 12pm to 5pm. Happy to help you find something special! 🍷
Perfect, I'll swing by Saturday afternoon then. Thanks!

Ciao Catherine, it's Sofia at Tenuta Marchetti. Our Barolo 2014 allocation for you is confirmed at 26 cases, received and counted in at your warehouse. Ready to move ahead on the distribution agreement whenever Richard's review is complete.

Grazie Sofia! 26 cases matches our dock count. I'm reviewing the landed cost with the tariff before we finalize. Once Richard clears the agreement I'd like to talk about adding four to five more Piedmont and Tuscan estates over the next couple of years.
Perfetto. I'll have our export team send the updated pricing and terms for the wider Barolo and Barbaresco program so you can share them with Richard. Looking forward to building this together.
Hi, I made a payment yesterday for my order but haven't received a receipt yet. Could you please send it over? — David Kim
Hello David! Apologies for the delay. Your receipt for order #AW-2847 has just been emailed to the address on file. Please check your spam folder too!
Got it, thank you! Just found it in spam. Much appreciated.
Sofia Marchetti confirmed the Barolo 2014 allocation - 3 cases, arriving January.
The Meritage Room wants 10 cases of the Brunello di Montalcino. Holding 31 for now.
Susan Bradley, the Chianti Classico shipment cleared customs. Delivering to Bouchon Yountville Wednesday.
Tasting note on the Amarone della Valpolicella 2017: floral nose, mineral core.
Invoice for Auberge du Soleil is ready - Gavi di Gavi, 31 cases.
Can we move up the Soave Classico delivery? The French Laundry account has a tasting Monday.
Cantina Rossi raised the Barolo price 4% for 2020. Reviewing margins.
Reserve list update: 12 cases Brunello di Montalcino 2021 back in the warehouse.
Tenuta Colombaia confirmed the Napa Valley Cabernet 2014 allocation - 19 cases, arriving March.
Bistro Jeanty wants 26 cases of the Russian River Pinot Noir. Holding 7 for now.
Eric Hoffman, the Franciacorta Brut shipment cleared customs. Delivering to Ciccio Napa Monday.
Tasting note on the Alto Adige Riesling 2017: elegant, restrained oak.
Invoice for The Meritage Room is ready - Barbaresco, 7 cases.
Can we move up the Vino Nobile di Montepulciano delivery? Bouchon Yountville has a tasting Thursday.
Marchetti Vini raised the Barbaresco price 4% for 2020. Reviewing margins.
Reserve list update: 28 cases Vermentino 2021 back in the warehouse.
Maison Bertrand confirmed the Vino Nobile di Montepulciano 2014 allocation - 35 cases, arriving November.
Farmstead wants 42 cases of the Vintage Port. Holding 23 for now.
Brian Harper, the Gavi di Gavi shipment cleared customs. Delivering to Mustards Grill Thursday.
Tasting note on the Montepulciano d'Abruzzo 2017: silky tannins, drinking beautifully now.
Invoice for Bistro Jeanty is ready - Chiaretto Rose, 23 cases.
Can we move up the Nebbiolo Langhe delivery? Ciccio Napa has a tasting Tuesday.
